name: Soviet Union
funFact: The hammer and sickle represented the union of industrial workers and peasants.

name: German Empire
funFact: The black-white-red tricolor flew over a unified Germany until the empire fell in 1918.

name: Ottoman Empire
funFact: Its star and crescent influenced the flags of many modern nations across the Middle East.

name: Austria-Hungary
funFact: The dual monarchy combined the colors of both the Austrian and Hungarian crowns.

name: Yugoslavia
funFact: The red star marked Tito's socialist federation of six Balkan republics.

name: Russian Empire
funFact: This black-yellow-white 'imperial' flag was used for official occasions under the Tsars.

name: East Germany
funFact: The emblem of hammer, compass and rye distinguished it from West Germany's plain tricolor.

name: British India
funFact: The 'Star of India' ensign represented British colonial rule before independence in 1947.

name: Canada
funFact: Canada flew the Red Ensign until the iconic maple-leaf flag was adopted in 1965.

name: South Africa
funFact: This 'oranje-blanje-blou' flag was replaced by the rainbow flag at the end of apartheid in 1994.

name: United States
funFact: For a time the U.S. flag had 15 stars and 15 stripes — the only design with more than 13 stripes.

name: Kingdom of Italy
funFact: The Savoy royal arms sat at the center until Italy became a republic in 1946.

name: Spanish Republic
img: Flag of the Second Spanish Republic.svg
funFact: The red-yellow-purple tricolor flew over Spain's short-lived Second Republic.

name: Kingdom of Greece
funFact: A crowned cross design served Greece through monarchy and republic for over a century.

name: Imperial Iran
funFact: The Lion and Sun emblem flew under the Shah until the 1979 revolution changed the flag.

name: Kingdom of Libya
funFact: This red-black-green flag with a crescent returned to Libya in 2011 after Gaddafi's fall.

name: Libya
funFact: Gaddafi's all-green flag was the only national flag in the world of a single solid color.

name: Burma
funFact: Myanmar flew this socialist-era flag with rice and gears until a new design in 2010.

name: Rhodesia
funFact: Rhodesia became Zimbabwe in 1980, adopting an entirely new flag.

name: South Vietnam
funFact: The yellow flag with three red stripes ceased to fly when Vietnam reunified in 1975.

name: Socialist Romania
funFact: Revolutionaries cut the communist emblem out of the flag during the 1989 uprising.

name: Hungarian People's Republic
funFact: The Rákosi coat of arms was torn from flags during the 1956 revolution.

name: Kingdom of Egypt
funFact: A green flag with a crescent and three stars marked Egypt's monarchy era.

name: Kingdom of Iraq
funFact: The Hashemite monarchy's flag bore a red trapezoid with two white stars.

name: Iraq
funFact: The takbir 'God is great' was added to the flag during the Gulf War era.

name: United Arab Republic
img: Flag of the United Arab Republic (1958–1971).svg
funFact: Egypt and Syria briefly merged into one state, symbolised by two green stars.

name: Empire of Brazil
funFact: An imperial crown crowned the green-and-gold flag until Brazil became a republic in 1889.

name: Ceylon
funFact: Ceylon was renamed Sri Lanka in 1972, later updating its lion flag.

name: Zaire
funFact: Mobutu's Zaire — a hand holding a torch — is today the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

name: Upper Volta
funFact: Upper Volta was renamed Burkina Faso — 'land of upright people' — in 1984.

name: Kingdom of Afghanistan
funFact: Afghanistan has changed its national flag more times than almost any other country.

name: Republic of Texas
img: Flag of the Republic of Texas (1836–1839).svg
funFact: Texas was an independent republic for nearly a decade before joining the United States.

name: Tibet
funFact: The snow-lion flag is still used by the Tibetan government-in-exile today.

name: Biafra
funFact: Biafra was a breakaway state in south-eastern Nigeria during a three-year civil war.
